column1: **Sales & customer intelligence platform for multi-region e-commerce — Python ETL, RFM segmentation, cohort retention, demand forecasting, and Power BI / Tableau-ready exports.**

RetailPulse simulates a realistic 2-year, multi-region e-commerce operation (10 stores across India, 4,000 customers, 50K+ transaction line items with genuine seasonality — Diwali spikes, monsoon slowdowns) and runs it through a full analytics pipeline:
- **ETL** — raw transactions → validated star schema (`fact_sales` + 4 dimension tables)
- **RFM segmentation** — every customer scored on Recency/Frequency/Monetary and bucketed into Champions, At Risk, Lost, etc.
- **Cohort retention analysis** — month-over-month retention heatmap by signup cohort
- **Demand forecasting** — 90-day revenue forecast via Holt-Winters exponential smoothing, backtested
- **BI-tool exports** — the same star schema imports cleanly into **Power BI** or **Tableau** (see [`docs/POWER_BI_GUIDE.md`](docs/POWER_BI_GUIDE.md))
- **Interactive dashboard** — a self-contained HTML console (`dashboard/index.html`) if you don't have BI software installed
Open [`dashboard/index.html`](dashboard/index.html) in any browser — no server or build step required.
- Diwali season (Oct–Nov) drives ~1.6x baseline daily revenue; monsoon months (Jun–Jul) soften demand
- The **Champions** segment (~21% of customers) drives the largest share of revenue — a clear retention-campaign target
- Retention decays sharply after month 1 for most cohorts, suggesting a day-30 win-back offer could meaningfully lift LTV
- 90-day Holt-Winters forecast, backtested MAPE ≈ 21% on a 30-day holdout
Full walkthrough with charts and commentary: [`notebooks/RetailPulse_Analysis.ipynb`](notebooks/RetailPulse_Analysis.ipynb)
│   ├── powerbi/                # star schema CSVs for Power BI
└── .github/workflows/ci.yml    # automated pipeline + test run on push
open dashboard/index.html        # or just double-click it
To explore in Jupyter: `jupyter notebook notebooks/RetailPulse_Analysis.ipynb`
To import into Power BI or Tableau, see [`docs/POWER_BI_GUIDE.md`](docs/POWER_BI_GUIDE.md).
`Python` · `pandas` · `numpy` · `statsmodels` (Holt-Winters forecasting) · `matplotlib` · `Plotly.js` · `pytest` · `GitHub Actions` · Power BI / Tableau-ready star schema exports

This project uses a **synthetic dataset** (see `data/generate_data.py`) generated with realistic seasonality, regional variance, and customer churn patterns for portfolio/demonstration purposes — no real customer or transaction data is used.
